steps.title: Determine the structure of f(x) using the doubling constraint
steps.content:
For an exponential function $f(x) = a \cdot b^{kx}$, when $x$ increases by $c$, we have:
$$f(x+c) = a \cdot b^{k(x+c)} = a \cdot b^{kx} \cdot b^{kc} = f(x) \cdot b^{kc}$$

Since $f(x)$ increases by a factor of 27 when $x$ increases by $c$, we need:
$$b^{kc} = 27$$

If the coefficient of $x$ is $\frac{1}{c}$, then $k = \frac{1}{c}$, so:
$$b^{\frac{1}{c} \cdot c} = b^1 = 27$$

Therefore, the correct form must have base 27 with coefficient $\frac{1}{c}$ of $x$.

steps.title: Identify which form displays 27 as the base with the coefficient 1/c
steps.content:
Check each form for base 27:
  • A: $(3)^{\frac{1}{2}x}$ — base is 3, not 27
  • B: $(3^{3})^{\frac{1}{6}x} = (27)^{\frac{1}{6}x}$ — base is explicitly 27, coefficient of $x$ is $\frac{1}{6}$ ✓
  • C: $(9)^{\frac{1}{4}x}$ — base is 9, not 27
  • D: $(27^{\frac{1}{3}x})^{\frac{1}{2}} = 27^{\frac{1}{6}x}$ — simplifies to base 27, but the coefficient $\frac{1}{6}$ is not directly displayed
Choice B directly displays $(27)^{\frac{1}{6}x}$, which means $\frac{1}{c} = \frac{1}{6}$, so $c = 6$.

steps.title: Verify the constraint is satisfied
steps.content: With $c = 6$: $$f(x+6) = 73 \cdot 27^{\frac{1}{6}(x+6)} = 73 \cdot 27^{\frac{x}{6} + 1} = 73 \cdot 27^{\frac{x}{6}} \cdot 27^1 = 27 \cdot f(x)$$ When $x$ increases by 6, $f(x)$ multiplies by 27. ✓ That matches choice B — B is correct.
